latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Start a new Brief as draft.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: On New Brief (/new-brief), when a Client lands on the page a Brief is auto-created with status=draft, scoped to the Client’s single Organization. The draft supports editing project-level fields: title (optional until submission), description, priority (default=normal), desiredDueDate, budget, creativeDirection, metadata. submittedBy is set to the creator’s user ID at draft creation. Cancel always deletes the auto-created draft so no draft remains. Visibility: Clients in the org can see the draft; Producers assigned to that org (via ProducerAssignment) have view-only access.

in: Autosave-on-entry creation of a draft Brief on /new-brief (status=draft, org-scoped, submittedBy=creator, priority=normal)
Editable project-level fields: title (optional in draft), description, priority, desiredDueDate, budget, creativeDirection, metadata
Continuous autosave of field edits after initial create
Visibility: Clients in the owning Organization can view the draft; assigned Producers have view-only access
Cancel action that deletes the auto-created draft
out: Submitting a Brief (status changes beyond draft)
UI for managing references content (backend defaulting only)

assumptions: Each user belongs to exactly one Organization; no org selector is shown.
Title is optional in draft; backend stores a placeholder value at create time (e.g., "Untitled Brief") due to non-null title column.
Priority defaults to normal unless changed by the Client.
Autosave persists edits for all listed project-level fields.
Brief.metadata and Brief.references initialize to empty objects if not provided.
Budget accepts any numeric value without additional validation.
Producers have view-only access to drafts for their assigned client orgs in this story.
acceptanceCriteria: When a Client navigates to /new-brief, a Brief is immediately persisted with status=draft, orgId set to the user’s Organization, submittedBy set to the current user, priority=normal, and non-null defaults for required fields (e.g., title placeholder, metadata={}, references={}).
The draft allows editing title, description, priority, desiredDueDate, budget, creativeDirection, and metadata; changes are autosaved.
Title may remain empty in the UI while the Brief is in draft; it is only required at submission (submission not in scope).
Cancel invoked from /new-brief deletes the auto-created draft so that no draft from that session exists afterward.
Only Clients who are members of the owning Organization can retrieve and view the draft.
Rivvy Producers assigned to the owning Organization via ProducerAssignment can view the draft but cannot edit it.
No BriefItems or Deliverables are created or modified as part of this story.
No emails or notifications are sent as part of this story.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Start a new Brief as draft

increments.title: Autosave draft creation, edit, visibility, and cancel-delete for New Brief
increments.intent:
- Backend (Portal API: ifc-mm2fbrdg-s9zlg)
  - Data model usage:
    - Use table rivvy_portal.brief (id, org_id, submitted_by, title, description, status, priority, desired_due_date, budget, creative_direction, references, metadata, created_at, updated_at).
    - Apply server-side defaults at create: status='draft', priority='normal', title='Untitled Brief', references={}, metadata={}. submitted_by=current user, org_id=current user’s org.
  - Authorization & visibility:
    - Determine org via OrganizationMember. Enforce role-based access using OrgRoleAssignment and ProducerAssignment.
    - CLIENTs in the brief’s org: can POST (create) and PATCH while status='draft', GET, and DELETE their own draft.
    - PRODUCERs with ProducerAssignment(client_org_id == brief.org_id): can GET and list drafts (view-only); cannot PATCH/DELETE.
    - Others: 403 or 404 as appropriate.
  - Endpoints (new under Portal API):
    - POST /api/briefs
      - Create draft on page entry with defaults above. Returns 201 + Brief JSON.
    - PATCH /api/briefs/{id}
      - Autosave partial updates while status='draft'. Allowed: title (non-empty only), description, priority, desiredDueDate, budget, creativeDirection, metadata. Validate types; ignore empty/whitespace title to preserve placeholder.
    - DELETE /api/briefs/{id}
      - Cancel deletes the draft. Allowed only to creator while status='draft'. Return 204 on success; 409 if not draft.
    - GET /api/briefs/{id}
      - Fetch details. CLIENT in org or assigned PRODUCER allowed; PRODUCER is view-only.
    - GET /api/briefs?status=draft (optional/recommended)
      - List visible drafts for current user (CLIENT: own org; PRODUCER: assigned orgs).
  - Error handling:
    - 401 unauthenticated; 403 unauthorized; 404 not found/cross-org; 409 invalid state (edit/delete non-draft); 422 invalid payload.
  - Serialization mapping:
    - API ↔ DB: title, description, priority, desiredDueDate↔desired_due_date, budget, creativeDirection↔creative_direction, metadata, references, status, orgId↔org_id, submittedBy↔submitted_by, createdAt/updatedAt.

- Frontend (UI screen: ui-mmkz7g4w-5k4mf /new-brief)
  - On mount: POST /api/briefs; hold loading until ID returned; store briefId and initial values.
  - Form fields: Title (optional in draft), Description, Priority (low|normal|high|urgent; default normal), Desired Due Date (date picker), Budget (numeric), Creative Direction, Metadata (lightweight JSON/key-value editor or textarea backing a JSON object).
  - Title UX: If server returns placeholder, render input empty; do not send title in PATCH until user types non-empty value.
  - Autosave: debounce ~500ms on change/blur; PATCH allowed fields; show subtle saved indicator; retry on transient failures.
  - Cancel: confirm, then DELETE /api/briefs/{id}; on success navigate to /dashboard; if delete fails, show error and allow retry.
  - Route guard: restrict /new-brief to CLIENT users; PRODUCERs won’t navigate here in normal flow.

- Security enforcement
  - Backend policies for role and org checks on all endpoints; PRODUCERs strictly read-only for drafts.

- Data consistency & defaults
  - Server enforces placeholder title, status/priority defaults, and non-null JSONB for references/metadata. Ignore empty title on PATCH.

- Testing
  - Backend tests: POST creates draft with correct org/submittedBy/defaults; PATCH enforces fields and ignores empty title; GET respects visibility; DELETE only by creator and only when draft; list filters by role/org.
  - Frontend tests: initial POST on mount; autosave debounce behavior; title placeholder UX; cancel-delete flow redirects; access guard behavior.

- Observability & rollout
  - Structured logs on create/update/delete with brief_id, org_id, user_id, outcome. Optional feature flag to gate autosave create flow.

answers.b66db736-fc74-4830-ad6f-71039e636234: There is only one org per user

messages.content: Welcome—ready to support the “Start a new Brief as draft” story. I have the product backlog and full architecture model on hand; would you like to highlight any specific entities or diagrams (e.g., New Brief screen, Brief/Organization data, Portal API) as most relevant?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my understanding of “Start a new Brief as draft”; I have 6 questions to confirm details.

Answers to your questions:

Q: When is the draft created—only on explicit Save/Start, or immediately upon landing on /new-brief (autosave)?
A: autosave

Q: Should title be optional in draft (enforced only at submit), matching the story’s wording?
A: Yes

Q: For users in multiple orgs, do we always use the current org context, or allow explicit org selection when starting a draft?
A: There is only one org per user

Q: What should Brief.submittedBy contain while in draft—the creator’s user ID now, or null until submission?
A: creator’s user ID 

Q: Any constraints or currency handling for budget to validate at draft-save time?
A: None

Q: For Producers who can see drafts, is access view-only in this story, or can they edit draft fields?
A: access view-only
Thanks—updated understanding captured with criteria. I have 1 question to finalize.

Answers to your questions:

Q: With autosave creating a draft on entry, what should Cancel do—leave the draft as-is, delete it only if it has no user-entered content, or always delete it?
A: always delete it
Thanks—updated with autosave and cancel-delete behavior. I’m ready to proceed to spec.
